 scenario 1 two players choose different numbers

player a choose any number between 1 and 5
 player b can only choose one number out of the remaining four

outcome for player b
 if player a chooses a specific number say  x  then
   if  x  1  player b wins 1
   if  x  2  player b loses 1
   if  x  3  player b wins 1
   if  x  4  player b loses 1
   if  x  5  player b wins 1

 scenario 2 two players choose the same number

scenario 2a both players choose the same number

if both players choose the same number lets call it  y

 player b choose  y 

outcome for player a

 if player a chooses another number player b loses 1

outcome for player b
 if player b chooses  y  player a wins 1
 if player b chooses another number player a loses 1

best choice for player a
 since player a always wins when chosen the best choice for player a is to choose a number different from the number chosen by player b

example strategy
 player a can choose 1 2 3 or 4
 player b can choose any number from 1 to 5

outcome analysis
 if player a chooses 1 player b loses 1
 if player a chooses 2 player b wins 1
 if player a chooses 3 player b wins 1
 if player a chooses 4 player b wins 1
 if player a chooses 5 player b loses 1

thus the optimal choice for player a depends on the outcome of player bs choice for example if player b chooses 1 player a should choose 2 if player b chooses 2 player a should choose 3 etc

 conclusion

for two players choosing different numbers
 best option for player a always choose a different number than player b
 best option for player b always choose a different number than player a

for two players choosing the same number
 best option for player a always choose a different number than player b
 best option for player b always choose a different number than player a

this analysis shows how to coordinate your choices effectively ensuring a fair game regardless of who goes first
